data.content:
Prompt Caching
A feature-based method that reduces the cost of Contextual Retrieval by allowing a reference document to be loaded into cache once and reused for subsequent chunk processing.

Contextual Retrieval
A retrieval method described as uniquely possible at low cost with Claude when combined with prompt caching to generate contextualized chunks.

Claude
A system or tool that enables Contextual Retrieval at low cost through a special prompt caching feature.

Reference Document
A document that does not need to be passed for every chunk when prompt caching is used, because it can be loaded into cache once and referenced later.

Cache
A storage mechanism into which the reference document is loaded once so that previously cached content can be referenced.

Chunk
A segment of content processed in contextual retrieval, assumed to contain 800 tokens and receive 100 tokens of context.

Document Tokens
A token-based measurement of document size used to express the one-time cost of generating contextualized chunks per million document tokens.

Context Instructions
Instructions associated with context generation, assumed to contain 50 tokens in the cost example.

Contextualized Chunks
Chunks produced through the contextual retrieval process using cached reference content and context additions.

One-Time Cost
The stated cost to generate contextualized chunks, given as $1.02 per million document tokens under the provided assumptions.

$1.02 Per Million Document Tokens
The stated one-time cost to generate contextualized chunks under the given assumptions.

800 Token Chunks
The assumed chunk size used in the cost calculation for generating contextualized chunks.

8k Token Documents
The assumed document size used in the cost calculation for generating contextualized chunks.

50 Token Context Instructions
The assumed size of context instructions used in the cost calculation.

100 Tokens Of Context Per Chunk
The assumed amount of context added to each chunk in the cost calculation.
